actionable_feedback: Provide parseable exports of the workbook's key sheets (Summary, Inputs, Analysis, Decision, Validation) as CSV or JSON files and attach them to the attempt (e.g., [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] ...).
If possible, rerun artifact-generation locally with openpyxl available and attach the resulting parsed outputs (or screenshots) for critical formulas so reviewers can validate formulas directly.

Clarify the provenance of the semantic_score in metadata: explicitly state whether the semantic score is 'model-judged, based on raw_actual_outputs' or 'verified by spreadsheet parsing'. Remove or mark as provisional if not substantiated by parsed artifact.

Add an explicit mapping table in the workbook (or as a separate CSV) mapping backlog row IDs to worksheet cells (e.g., BacklogID -> Inputs!A2), to make traceability unambiguous for automated verifiers.

Include a short sampled recomputation checklist in the Analysis or Validation sheet that shows formula text (or formula screenshots) and one or two worked examples used to compute priority/remediation order when spreadsheet parsing tools are unavailable.
Add a small sensitivity table (e.g., findings +/-1) in the uncertainty_and_assumptions sheet showing which remediation orders would change to strengthen reviewer confidence in stability of ranking.
Update the attempt metadata to note that semantic assertions are provisional until parsed artifacts are provided; reference the verifier issues (V-1, V-2).
artifact_specific_comments: Artifact preview parse_status = "openpyxl_unavailable" with preview_error_message "No module named 'openpyxl'" - attach exported CSV/JSON to enable automated verification.
Consider also exporting the decision_table as JSON/CSV to make sheet-to-file mappings unambiguous (grader suggestion).
feedback_summary: Deliverable artifact present and deterministic artifact-contract precheck passed. However, the model grader reported perfect semantic scores despite the environment being unable to parse the XLSX (openpyxl_unavailable). The verifier flagged two issues: (V-1) semantic scoring asserted without spreadsheet parsing and (V-2) inconsistency between deterministic_precheck metadata (semantic_correctness_not_evaluated) and the model grader's reported semantic_score=1.0. Several suggested improvements from the grader (CSV/JSON exports, mapping table, recomputation checklist, sensitivity table) remain useful for reviewer confidence.

recommendations_quicklist: Attach CSV/JSON exports for Summary, Inputs, Analysis, Decision, Validation sheets.
Include a mapping table: backlog_row_id -> workbook cell references.
Add sampled recomputation examples (formula text/screenshots) for critical rule applications.
Add sensitivity table for findings thresholds in uncertainty_and_assumptions.
Update metadata to clarify semantic scoring provenance.

verifier_issues.description: Deterministic precheck metadata indicates semantic correctness not evaluated, but model grader reports semantic_score = 1.0. Clarify whether scoring used only raw_actual_outputs or real artifact parsing.
verifier_issues.suggested_action: Either perform artifact parsing to substantiate semantic scores or mark the semantic score as 'model-judged based on raw_actual_outputs' and update metadata.
verifier_issues.title: Inconsistency between deterministic precheck and model semantic scoring
